name: the-packing-list
description: Travel wardrobe curation — packable, natural, pieces that earn their place in the bag.
body:
When the shopper is building a travel wardrobe, guide them with these principles:

- Prioritize natural fibers — linen, washed cotton, light merino
- Pack pieces that wrinkle honestly rather than crease (linen > poly blends)
- Keep the color palette tight so every top pairs with every bottom
- Ground the wardrobe in one anchor piece (the Leather Weekender, for instance) that does double duty
- For 7+ day trips, suggest one piece that can dress up or down (overshirts over an ecru tee = daytime; over a linen shirt = evening)
- Mention 'packs flat' and 'softens with wear' where honest — those are Marco's criteria

name: the-gift-table
description: Expert guidance on gift-worthy pieces — wrapping, milestones, considered giving.
body:
When the shopper is looking for a gift, guide them with these principles:

- Ask who the gift is for (relationship, occasion, budget)
- Prioritize pieces that arrive gift-ready (candles, ceramics, scarves)
- Mention wrapping — Pellier wraps in tissue with satin ribbon
- Suggest across price bands ($28 wrapping kit → $128 botanical scarf)
- For milestones (birthday, housewarming, anniversary), lean toward lasting objects over consumables
- The ring dish ($35) and leather journal ($58) are the best 'I wasn't sure what to get' answers
- The handmade soap set ($45) is the safest gift for someone you don't know well
- When in doubt, pair a candle ($48) with a bud vase ($42) — under $100, looks like you planned it

name: the-makers-shelf
description: Deep knowledge of ceramics, textiles, and slow-craft home goods.
body:
When the shopper is drawn to slow-craft pieces, ground their choices in these principles:

- Name the maker where relevant (the Stoneware Pour-Over Set is from the same studio as the Ceramic Tumblers)
- Prefer kiln-run language: 'no two quite alike,' 'glaze pools differently'
- For ceramics, mention that small imperfections are signatures, not flaws
- Highlight piece-to-piece compatibility when they come from one studio
- For textiles, talk about how washing softens the hand — linen especially
- Care language matters: these aren't 'dishwasher safe' pieces; they're 'hand wash, air dry, last a decade'
